source: Open Trivia Database (CC BY-SA 4.0) + hand-drafted facts
note: Bootstrapped with hand-drafted exemplars across categories. Run scripts/audit-questions.py to grow toward the 200 target.

questions.prompt: The Flag of the European Union has how many stars on it?
questions.fact: The 12 stars on the EU flag don't represent member countries.

questions.prompt: Antibiotics are generally taken to combat what?
questions.fact: Antibiotics only work against bacteria

questions.prompt: In which country is the city of Rio de Janeiro?
questions.fact: Brazil. Rio de Janeiro means 'River of January' in Portuguese.

questions.prompt: The moons, Miranda, Ariel, Umbriel, Titania and Oberon orbit which planet?
questions.fact: Uranus's moons are mostly named after characters from Shakespeare and Alexander Pope.

questions.prompt: Which continent do sweet potatoes originally come from?
questions.fact: Sweet potatoes were domesticated in South America at least 8,000 years ago.

questions.prompt: What country hosted the 2014 Winter Olympics?
questions.fact: Sochi 2014 was the most expensive Olympics in history at the time.

questions.prompt: How many legs do butterflies have?
questions.fact: All insects have six legs, and butterflies are insects.

questions.prompt: The word "aprosexia" means which of the following?
choices: The inability to concentrate on anything
A feverish desire to rip one's clothes off
questions.fact: The inability to concentrate or focus attention.

questions.prompt: What is a "dakimakura"?
choices: A Chinese meal, essentially composed of fish
A word used to describe two people who truly love each other
questions.fact: A dakimakura is a large, human-sized body pillow from Japan.

questions.prompt: In what year did Halley's Comet, which will not appear again until the year 2061, previously approach Earth?
questions.fact: Halley's Comet last approached Earth in 1986.

questions.prompt: Rolex is a company that specializes in what type of product?
questions.fact: Rolex specializes in manufacturing high-end, luxury Swiss wristwatches.

questions.prompt: Which iconic Disneyland attraction was closed in 2017 to be remodeled as a "Guardians of the Galaxy" themed ride?
questions.fact: Where random, faster-than-gravity thrills await inside the abandoned Hollywood Tower Hotel.

questions.prompt: What character was once considered to be the 27th letter of the alphabet?
questions.fact: It acts as a stylized, one-line substitute for "and"

questions.prompt: What company owns Outlook?
questions.fact: Part of the Microsoft 365 suite, formerly known as Hotmail

questions.prompt: Which product did Nokia, the telecommunications company, originally sell?
questions.fact: Nokia originally started in 1865 as a wood pulp mill producing paper.

questions.prompt: Directly between the Washington Monument and the Reflecting Pool is a memorial to which war?
questions.fact: Features 4,048 gold stars on a dark wall, representing over 400,000 Americans who died

questions.prompt: What is the largest rapid transit system in the world by number of stations, with 472 stations in operation?
questions.fact: The New York City Subway is the largest rapid transit system in the world by number of stations

questions.prompt: Which candy is NOT made by Mars?
questions.fact: Almond Joy is not made by Mars; it is manufactured by The Hershey Company.

questions.prompt: What year was the first Apple iPod introduced?
questions.fact: Was introduced on October 23, 2001, by Steve Jobs.

questions.prompt: What was the destination of the missing flight MH370?
questions.fact: Beijing Capital International Airport in China.

questions.prompt: What is the Italian word for "tomato"?
questions.fact: The Italian word for "tomato" is pomodoro.

questions.prompt: Which is the second largest native language spoken in Spain by numbers of speakers?
questions.fact: Catalan.

questions.prompt: Which country developed the AK-47 assault rifle?
questions.fact: The AK-47 assault rifle was developed in the Soviet Union (USSR).

questions.prompt: What is the first book of the Old Testament?
questions.fact: The name originates from Greek, meaning "origin" or "beginning,".

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not an Ivy League University?
questions.fact: While not an Ivy, Stanford is considered "Ivy Plus," placing it among the most elite institutions in the world.

questions.prompt: Amsterdam Centraal station is twinned with what station?
questions.fact: Amsterdam Centraal station is twinned with London Liverpool Street station.

questions.prompt: What did the Spanish autonomous community of Catalonia ban in 2010, that took effect in 2012?
questions.fact: In 2010, Catalonia banned bullfighting (effective 2012). Though Spain's high court overturned the ban in 2016, the practice has not returned

questions.prompt: In which country was the Michelin tire company founded in 1889?
questions.fact: The Michelin tire company was founded in 1889 in France.

questions.prompt: What was the name given to Japanese military dictators who ruled the country through the 12th and 19th Century?
questions.fact: Shogun a title meaning "barbarian-subduing commander-in-chief"

questions.prompt: Out of these four buildings, which one is the tallest, with a height of 1,250 ft (381 m)?
choices: Bank of China Tower, Hong Kong
questions.fact: It was the world's tallest building for 40 years

questions.prompt: Which restaurant's mascot is a clown?
questions.fact: McDonald's mascot is Ronald McDonald. This red-haired, yellow-suited icon debuted in 1963 to appeal to children globally.

questions.prompt: The tale of Robin Hood originates from which country?
questions.fact: The legend is rooted in English folklore, specifically tied to locations like Sherwood Forest

questions.prompt: What is "dabbing"?
questions.fact: The dab is a 2010s viral dance move originating from Atlanta hip-hop culture

questions.prompt: Area 51 is located in which US state?
questions.fact: Area 51 is a highly classified United States Air Force facility

questions.prompt: Which of the following presidents is not on Mount Rushmore?
questions.fact: Mount Rushmore National Memorial features the carved faces of only four U.S. presidents: George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, Theodore Roosevelt, and Abraham Lincoln.

questions.prompt: What is the name of the extra pedal on a manual or standard transmission car?
questions.fact: The clutch  is used to disengage the engine from the transmission, allowing the driver to change gears.

questions.prompt: What is real haggis made of?
questions.fact: Traditional haggis is a savory Scottish pudding made from sheep's heart, liver, and lungs, minced with onion, oatmeal, suet (fat), spices, and stock

questions.prompt: Which of the following is not another name for the eggplant?
questions.fact: Potimarron is a small, pear-shaped, French winter squash not eggplant.

questions.prompt: Which church's interior in Vatican City was designed in 1503 by renaissance architects including Bramante, Michelangelo and Bernini?
questions.fact: The largest, most iconic church is St. Peter's Basilica

questions.prompt: How many notes are there on a standard grand piano?
questions.fact: A standard grand piano has 88 keys, consisting of 52 white keys and 36 black keys

questions.prompt: According to the nursery rhyme, what fruit did Little Jack Horner pull out of his Christmas pie?
questions.fact: He put in his thumb, / And pulled out a plum, / And said, 'What a good boy am I

questions.prompt: Which canal connects the Mediterranean Sea with the Red Sea?
questions.fact: The Suez Canal is a 193-km (120-mile) artificial sea-level waterway in Egypt, serving as the critical, shortest maritime shortcut between Europe and Asia

questions.prompt: What is the longest bridge in the world?
choices: King Fahd Causeway Bridge I, Saudi Arabia
Sultan Abdul Halim Muadzam Shah Bridge, Malaysia
questions.fact: Completed in 2011, this massive structure on the Beijing-Shanghai High-Speed Railway spans approximately 164.8 kilometers (102.4 miles)

questions.prompt: Before the 19th Century, the "Living Room" was originally called the...
questions.fact: The Parlor was traditionally a formal space used for entertaining guests, holding social events

questions.prompt: What machine element is located in the center of fidget spinners?
questions.fact: Bearings are rolling-element components that reduce friction between moving parts

questions.prompt: Which of these words refers to something made, distributed, or sold illegally?
questions.fact: Bootleg refers to goods—traditionally alcohol, but now commonly computer software, recordings, or films—that are produced, distributed, or sold illegally

questions.prompt: Which of these American cities has fewer than 1,000,000 people?
questions.fact: A foggy, iconic coastal city defined by steep hills, Victorian charm, cable cars, and the stunning Golden Gate Bridge

questions.prompt: What was the original name of Ho Chi Minh City?
questions.fact: Saigon (Ho Chi Minh City) is a vibrant, fast-paced metropolis where motorbike-filled streets merge historic charm with rapid modern development

questions.prompt: Which European city has the highest mileage of canals in the world?
questions.fact: Birmingham has over 100 miles of canals, far exceeding Amsterdam (~60) and Venice (26).

questions.prompt: Where is the area commonly known as the Bermuda Triangle?
choices: North Pacific Ocean, between Japan and the USA
North Atlantic Ocean, between Florida and Puerto Rico
South Pacific Ocean, far off Chile
questions.fact: It is known for alleged, unproven mysteries, though it is a heavily traveled, non-hazardous region

questions.prompt: What is the state capital of South Dakota?
questions.fact: Pronounced "Peer," South Dakota's capital is a charming, small-town hub on the Missouri River

questions.prompt: What is the capital of Estonia?
questions.fact: Located on the northern coast along the Gulf of Finland, it is the country's largest city, primary financial hub

questions.prompt: How many countries share a land border with Russia? (Not including disputed territories)
questions.fact: These countries are: Norway, Finland, Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania, Poland, Belarus, Ukraine, Georgia, Azerbaijan, Kazakhstan, China, Mongolia, and North Korea.

questions.prompt: Which country is the home of the largest Japanese population outside of Japan?
questions.fact: An estimated 1.5 to over 2 million people of Japanese descent (nikkei) residing there

questions.prompt: The Pyrenees mountains are located on the border of which two countries?
questions.fact: This mountain range separates the Iberian Peninsula from the rest of Europe

questions.prompt: How tall is One World Trade Center in New York City?
questions.fact: It is the tallest building in the Western Hemisphere and was completed in 2014.

questions.prompt: Which Oceanian nation is known in Māori as Aotearoa?
questions.fact: Often translated as "Land of the Long White Cloud"

questions.prompt: What is the smallest country in the world?
questions.fact: This 121-acre city-state sits inside Rome, serves as the Catholic Church's center, and houses roughly 800 people.

questions.prompt: The Hunua Ranges is located in...
questions.fact: Located on the southeast side of the Auckland region on the North Island of New Zealand

questions.prompt: What mountain range lines the border between Spain and France?
questions.fact: Acts as the natural border between Spain and France

questions.prompt: The White Cliffs of Dover is located in which country?
questions.fact: They are situated along the coastline of Kent in southeast England, facing the Strait of Dover and France

questions.prompt: On a London Underground map, what colour is the Circle Line?
questions.fact: The line, which serves 36 stations in a loop around central London

questions.prompt: On which continent does the Andes mountain range lie?
questions.fact: The world's longest continental mountain range

questions.prompt: Harvard University is located in which city?
questions.fact: Is a historic and vibrant city renowned as an intellectual powerhouse, home to Harvard University and MIT

questions.prompt: Which Borough is the farthest in the north of New York City?
questions.fact: It is the only borough situated primarily on the United States mainland

questions.prompt: The country of Belize borders which country?
questions.fact: Bordered by Mexico to the north and northwest, and by Guatemala to the west and south

questions.prompt: What state is the largest state of the United States of America?
questions.fact: It's bigger than Texas, California, and Montana combined.

questions.prompt: What tiny principality lies between Spain and France?
questions.fact: Its two heads of state are the President of France and the Bishop of Urgell in Spain

questions.prompt: What is the land connecting North America and South America?
questions.fact: Panama connects North and South America and separates two oceans (Atlantic and Pacific)

questions.prompt: What is the name of the Canadian national anthem?
questions.fact: It is sung in both official languages (English and French)

questions.prompt: The land mass of modern day Turkey is called what?
questions.fact: It forms a large peninsula bridging Asia and Europe

questions.prompt: How many states are in Australia?
questions.fact: New South Wales, Queensland, South Australia, Tasmania, Victoria, and Western Australia

questions.prompt: What is the fifth largest country by area?
questions.fact: Brazil follows Russia, Canada, China, and the United States in size, ranking just ahead of Australia

questions.prompt: What North American tourist attraction is served by the "Maid of the Mist" tour company?
questions.fact: This historic attraction takes passengers from the Niagara River gorge into the mist of all three falls

questions.prompt: What is the capital of the U.S. state Texas?
questions.fact: Austin is known as the "Live Music Capital of the World"

questions.prompt: What is the capital of Australia?
questions.fact: It was founded in 1913 as a compromise between rivals Sydney and Melbourne

questions.prompt: What is the capital of Denmark?
questions.fact: It is known for its historic architecture, vibrant harbor (Nyhavn), and biking culture
